name: cloister
summary: A covered walk surrounding an open garth — a square or rectangular walkway of columns enclosing a small garden or well. Monks walk here in contemplation; plants, silence, and light shape the space.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: square — the enclosed garth is the heart, the walk the frame

accent.purpose: the central garth — grass, dirt, or gravel

primary_palette.note: a central well flanked by stone benches
a founder's effigy in the garth amid planted moss
an open-air font amid wild-growing greenery
a single sacred tree in the center of the garth

story_prompts: morning meditation — monks walking the perimeter in silence, garth unoccupied
a novice tending the garth — tools left against a pillar, work half-done
rain-soaked — the garth a shallow pool, the walkway glistening
a buried secret — one flagstone in the walkway lifted, something interred beneath
a quiet conspiracy — two figures stopped by the well, speaking too low to be heard

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: natural daylight in the garth (0.6+), dimmer shade beneath the walkway — a clear inside/outside contrast

anti_patterns: the walkway is continuous — you can circle the whole garth without interruption
the garth is open to the sky; never roof it over
avoid clutter in the walkway — monks must be able to walk single-file or in procession
